headline: GE Aerospace Targets Unmanned Growth With New US$12.4m Air Force Engine Contract
summary: GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) and Kratos Defense & Security Solutions announced a partnership under a new U.S. Air Force contract to develop the GEK1500 engine. The agreement focuses on next generation propulsion technology for unmanned platforms, including collaborative combat aircraft and unmanned aerial systems. The program is intended to move the GEK1500 from prototype toward scalable deployment for emerging defense applications. For GE Aerospace, this contract fits within its core focus on...

headline: A Look At General Electric’s Valuation As GE Aerospace Growth And Defense Engines Draw Investor Interest
summary: General Electric (GE) has been reshaped into GE Aerospace, and its latest US$12.4 million U.S. Air Force contract for the GEK1500 engine adds another data point for investors tracking this focused aerospace business. See our latest analysis for General Electric. After a strong 1-year total shareholder return of 68.83% and a very large 5-year total shareholder return, GE’s recent 7-day share price return of a 6.55% decline and modest 30-day gain suggest momentum has cooled slightly, even as...

headline: If You Invested $1,000 in Boeing or GE 5 Years Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Today
summary: Five years ago, you had $1,000 to invest and two famous American industrial names to choose from. Boeing (NYSE: BA) looked like the safer bet: a century-old aerospace giant, a dividend payer, a blue-chip name. General Electric, now GE Aerospace (NYSE: GE), looked like a cautionary tale, a conglomerate that had spent years destroying shareholder ... headline: GE Gains From Strength in Commercial Engines: Can the Momentum Sustain?
summary: GE Aerospace's Commercial Engines & Services revenues jump 24% in Q4 as strong engine demand and defense contracts drive growth.
